gap: No test covers the Kanban settings UI (add board, type property name, remove board). The `onchange` vs `oninput` save behavior, the stale-board autocomplete (initialBoards frozen at mount), and the duplicate-key board removal scenario are all untested.

gap: No test exercises `getHeadingsInBoard`, including the null return from `getFileCache` (which crashes the panel), the empty-headings case, or the 'FILE NOT FOUND' fallback. All headings (including archive/divider sections) being shown is also untested.

coveredBy: __tests__/kanbanHelper.test.ts: 'updates the target property based on the lane heading'; 'does not update when the property already matches the lane'; 'resolves the lane name from an ATX-closed heading'; 'resolves the lane name from a setext heading'; 'does not treat a wikilink inside a lane heading as a card' (lane name written verbatim)
gap: No test covers the case where `targetProperty.content` is null, undefined, or a number (non-string value already in frontmatter that differs from the lane name by type only), which always triggers an update on every save.

coveredBy: __tests__/kanbanHelper.test.ts: 'emits a notice naming the linked file when the tracked property is missing'; 'keeps updating later cards when an earlier card's property read throws'
gap: No test verifies the Notice spam scenario (rapid successive board saves each emit a Notice for the same missing-property card). The distinction between logMessage vs logError for different error types is untested.

coveredBy: __tests__/kanbanHelper.test.ts: 'only treats the leading link of a top-level task as a card link' (fixture builder fidelity); 'skips a link whose cached position no longer matches the board content'; 'never clobbers Kanban @[[date]] links'; 'does not treat a card whose text starts with prose then a link'; 'ignores indented sub-checklist items'
gap: The '- [ ][[Note]]' no-space case (CARD_LINK_PREFIX requires \s+ before the link) is acknowledged in AUDIT_NOTES but not explicitly tested. Multi-character checkbox content ('- [>>] [[Note]]') is also not directly tested.

coveredBy: __tests__/kanbanHelper.test.ts: 'resolves markdown links with encoded spaces and .md via metadata cache'; 'falls back to vault path resolution'; 'falls back to basename match'; 'does not fallback to basename when link includes a folder'
gap: The ambiguous-basename case (multiple notes with the same basename, first match wins arbitrarily) is not tested. The `decodeURIComponent` failure path (keeps partially-encoded string, silently returns null) is not tested.

coveredBy: e2e: 'adds a property to every note in a folder and is idempotent on re-run' (recursive collection + sub/c.md); e2e: 'collects a multi-selection without duplicates and wires the files-menu item' (dedup + TFile+TFolder selection); e2e: 'offers the folder bulk item when markdown lives only in a subfolder' (nested-only, no-markdown guard)
gap: No test for the zero-markdown-files early-return Notice path in run(). No test for a deeply nested folder (stack overflow risk). No unit test for collectFromFolder or collectFromSelection directly.

coveredBy: e2e: 'shows conflict options from live frontmatter when the metadata cache is stale' (exercises the key and value prompt via UI event dispatch)
gap: No test for cancel/empty-key abort path. No test for whitespace-only key (trim guard). No test for cancel on value prompt. No test verifying rawValue is passed without trimming (space-only value).

coveredBy: e2e: 'shows conflict options from live frontmatter when the metadata cache is stale' (stale cache; correct conflict count); e2e: 'does not abort conflict preflight on malformed frontmatter' (malformed YAML returns 0)
gap: No test for '...' YAML close marker; no test verifying the conflict count when a BOM is present; no test for cancel/dismiss on the conflict policy modal (null resolution).

coveredBy: e2e: 'adds a property to every note in a folder and is idempotent on re-run' (first run adds; second run skips); unit: 'decideBulkWrite - skip policy / never touches a note that already has the key'
gap: No test for skip policy on a note that has the key but with a null/empty value. No test verifying existing non-target properties are preserved (b.md status:draft survival is tested, but for a broader set of cases).

coveredBy: e2e: 'merge appends into a list without duplicating and converges on re-run'; unit: 'decideBulkWrite - merge policy' suite (appends, idempotent, scalar-normalize, type-dedup, map-skip, object-element preserve)
gap: No test for merge + EditMode.AllSingle (new notes incorrectly seeded as lists). No test for plain-object currentValue distinguishing 'skipped' from policy-skip in the summary. No test for merge on a null-valued existing key.

coveredBy: e2e: 'overwrite replaces existing values and is idempotent' (verifies overwrite logic and idempotency via apply() directly); e2e: 'shows conflict options from live frontmatter' (exercises the full two-modal confirmation UI flow)
gap: No test for overwrite + wrapInArray=true (existing list replaced by [scalar]). No test for the destructive confirmation being dismissed (Escape → abort). The 'files.length overstates blast radius' wording is not tested.

coveredBy: unit: 'decideBulkWrite - add / wraps the added value in a list when EditMode requests multi-value' (wrapInArray=true path); e2e: add/merge/overwrite tests exercise applyToFile indirectly
gap: No test for wrapInArrayFor() under SomeMulti where the key is in the properties list. No test for SomeMulti where the key is NOT in the list (scalar write). No integration test for AllMulti wrapping in the bulk write path.

coveredBy: unit: 'formatSummary' suite (non-zero buckets, failure hints, singular/plural, no-changes); unit: 'recordOutcome increments the matching bucket'
gap: No test for actual per-note failure isolation (i.e., processFrontMatter throwing on one file while others succeed). No test that failures array captures the correct path and error message. No e2e test for the 10-second Notice wording on a batch with failures.

coveredBy: none
gap: No test verifies that autoprop() opens the Auto Property modal programmatically and returns the selected value, that it returns null when Auto Properties is disabled, or that it returns null when no matching property exists.

coveredBy: tests/e2e/metaedit-runtime.test.ts — "creates, reads, and updates a YAML frontmatter property via the API"; "updates one numeric YAML property without changing its sibling"; "updates only the exact inline field key"; "preserves CRLF terminators when updating a full-line inline field" (covers YAML update; CRLF test covers the happy path only for YAML)
gap: The CRLF e2e test covers a YAML property update only. No test verifies CRLF preservation when api.update() targets a Dataview inline field (the split("\n")/join("\n") bug at metaController.ts:483-489). The silent-undefined return for a not-found property is also unverified.

coveredBy: none
gap: No test verifies getFilesWithProperty returns files correctly. The truthy-value exclusion bug (files with false/0/""/null values excluded) is entirely uncovered.

coveredBy: tests/e2e/metaedit-runtime.test.ts — "adds and updates properties through the public API" (covers addOrUpdateProperty for add and update cases); "serializes concurrent YAML property creation for one file" (covers createYamlProperty concurrency)
gap: No test verifies that createYamlProperty shows a Notice when the key already exists. The misplaced-period typo in the Notice text is unverified. addOrUpdateProperty's synthetic {key, type:YAML} fallback (which creates as a root key, not nested) is untested for dotted-key callers.

coveredBy: src/metaController.test.ts — all appendDataviewField unit tests cover the controller method directly; tests/e2e/metaedit-runtime.test.ts exercises addOrUpdateProperty which internally calls the controller
gap: No test calls api.appendDataviewField() through the public API wrapper. The location:'end' option via API is not e2e tested. CRLF preservation for appendDataviewField via the API (as opposed to the controller method directly) is uncovered at e2e level.

coveredBy: tests/e2e/metaedit-runtime.test.ts — "reads and updates existing nested YAML leaves through public APIs"; "keeps missing dotted legacy addOrUpdateProperty literal but explicit path writes can create parents"; "rejects scalar-parent and stale array nested writes without data loss"
gap: updateYamlPath error propagation to the caller (no Notice, raw rejection) is verified by the rejection tests but no test confirms the user sees no feedback notice. addOrUpdateYamlPath's createLeaf forwarding gap (createLeaf not passed) is unverified. MetaEditYamlPathOptions not exposing createLeaf is a design gap with no test.

coveredBy: tests/e2e/metaedit-runtime.test.ts — "reads and updates existing nested YAML leaves through public APIs" (getPropertyValue and getPropertiesInFile for existing files); "creates, reads, and updates a YAML frontmatter property via the API" (getPropertyValue happy path)
gap: No test verifies behavior when the file path argument cannot be resolved: getPropertiesInFile returns undefined (type violation) and getPropertyValue also returns undefined silently. The type mismatch for getPropertiesInFile(nonexistent-path) is entirely uncovered.

coveredBy: tests/e2e/metaedit-runtime.test.ts — "notifies metadata changes with parsed properties and cleans up subscriptions"; "keeps metadata change event data, cache, and properties in the same snapshot"; "metadata change events include a user frontmatter key named position"
gap: No test verifies rename event migrates previousProperties state. No test verifies the object-key-reordering spurious-callback issue (propertiesSignature instability). The deletion-event cleanup path is untested. Callback error isolation (callback throws, listener keeps running) is untested.

coveredBy: tests/e2e/metaedit-runtime.test.ts — "sets Auto Properties through the public API without exposing mutable settings" (covers setAutoProperties validation, rollback, cloning, and getAutoProperties cloning)
gap: No test verifies that a TypeError from setAutoProperties propagates as an unhandled rejection to external callers who do not catch. Empty-name and empty-choices entries being accepted silently is unverified. Concurrent setAutoProperties calls (race through settingsWriteQueue) is untested.

coveredBy: src/Settings/settingsMigration.test.ts: 'returns the defaults for a fresh install (null)', 'does not return the frozen default objects by reference', 'never aliases the mutable default arrays'
gap: No test verifies that UIElements.enabled defaults to true (the only non-false default) while all others default to false. No test verifies the exact values of every top-level section key simultaneously for a fresh install.

coveredBy: src/Settings/settingsMigration.test.ts: 'backfills a new nested field (hideFileTags)', 'backfills an entire section missing from stored data', 'lets stored scalars and arrays win wholesale', 'preserves unknown top-level keys', 'preserves an explicit hideFileTags value'; migrateIgnoredProperties suite: 4 migration scenarios
gap: No test for the case where a stored section value is a non-plain-object (null, array, scalar) — the merge silently falls back to default. No test verifies loadSettings is strictly read-only (no saveSettings call) for post-migration data.

coveredBy: none
gap: No tests for any feature toggle: Progress toggle calling toggleAutomators, Kanban toggle calling toggleAutomators, UIElements toggle calling linkMenu.registerEvent/unregisterEvent, Edit Meta toggle calling this.display(), same-value guard preventing redundant work. All toggle paths are untested.

coveredBy: none
gap: No tests for toggleHiddenEl (undefined arg no-op), gear-button click expanding/collapsing a sub-panel, bDivToggle state tracking in the Edit Mode section, or the dead-gear-button scenario when IgnoredProperties is disabled.

coveredBy: none
gap: No tests for destroySvelteElements being called on display(), the hide() override calling destroySvelteElements, saveSettings error propagation, or the lack of error handling around saveData failures.

coveredBy: tests/e2e/bulk-metadata.test.ts — 'offers the folder bulk item when markdown lives only in a subfolder' (calls onMenuOpenCallback with a TFile and asserts menu items); tests/e2e/menu-filter.test.ts — 'hides body #tags while keeping frontmatter...' (calls plugin.runMetaEditForFile, the function invoked by the Edit Meta click)
gap: No test verifies the exact file targeted when the right-click callback fires: the e2e test drives onMenuOpenCallback directly with a constructed menu sink and asserts the menu title, but never actually triggers the onClick handler and confirms which file's suggester opened. The stale-targetFile scenario (two rapid right-clicks) is untested.

coveredBy: tests/e2e/bulk-metadata.test.ts — 'offers the folder bulk item when markdown lives only in a subfolder' (calls onMenuOpenCallback with a TFolder and asserts the bulk-folder menu item appears; also verifies a markdown-free subfolder produces no item)
gap: No test actually clicks the folder bulk item and verifies it runs on the correct folder. The stale-targetFolder scenario (two rapid folder right-clicks) is untested. The zero-files early-return path (folder deleted between menu open and click) is untested.

coveredBy: tests/e2e/bulk-metadata.test.ts — 'collects a multi-selection without duplicates and wires the files-menu item' (calls onFilesMenuOpenCallback with two .md files and verifies the menu item appears; calls collectFromSelection with a folder+file and checks dedup)
gap: No test verifies the scopeLabel passed to bulkEditor.run(): the scope string shown in the property-name prompt is never asserted. The bug where selection.length (raw item count) diverges from files.length (resolved .md count) is untested.

coveredBy: none
gap: No test exercises the UIElements settings toggle: enabling when disabled, disabling when enabled, or the off->on->off->on sequence. No test verifies that right-click menus disappear after disabling UIElements, or that they appear after enabling from a disabled start state.

coveredBy: tests/e2e/bulk-metadata.test.ts — 'offers the folder bulk item when markdown lives only in a subfolder' (verifies that a markdown-free folder 'side/' produces no bulk-folder menu item via onMenuOpenCallback)
gap: No test covers non-markdown file types (canvas, pdf, png) individually. No test covers the performance/jank risk of folderHasMarkdown on a deeply-nested vault. No test verifies that the file-explorer item is absent for a .canvas file.
